If you were deploying a brand-new Linux server to production, what steps would you take to secure it before making it publicly accessible?
It's 2:00 AM and you're the on-call DevOps engineer. Your production Linux server suddenly becomes unreachable through SSH. Walk me through how you would investigate and restore the service.

Users report that a production application has become extremely slow. You log into the Linux server and notice one process consuming nearly all CPU resources. How would you identify the cause and resolve the issue?
A routine system update finishes successfully, but after rebooting, the Linux server fails to start correctly. Describe your troubleshooting process.
A teammate accidentally deletes an important configuration file on a production server. What immediate actions would you take to minimize downtime?
Your monitoring system alerts you that disk usage on a production server has reached 100%. Explain how you would investigate the issue and safely recover disk space.
A Linux service keeps crashing every few minutes, but no obvious error messages appear on the screen. Describe how you would diagnose the root cause.
A newly hired developer cannot access a project directory on a Linux server, even though they were added to the correct team. How would you troubleshoot the problem?
While reviewing authentication logs, you notice hundreds of failed SSH login attempts from multiple countries. What actions would you take to secure the server?
An application starts correctly when launched manually, but it fails every time the server reboots. Explain how you would investigate and permanently fix the issue.

During routine monitoring, you discover an unfamiliar process consuming significant system resources on a production server. How would you determine whether it is a legitimate process or a security threat?
